pick.rationale: Virginia and Tennessee are statistical equals with nearly identical advanced metrics, as the Cavaliers rank 13th/14th nationally compared to the Volunteers' 15th/15th marks with efficiency margins separated by less than half a point. Virginia's 29-4 record and ACC championship pedigree demonstrate slightly better consistency than Tennessee's 22-11 SEC campaign, while both teams feature elite defenses ranked 13th-14th nationally with comparable offensive profiles. While Tennessee enters with Nate Ament returning to form after his ankle injury, Virginia's superior win rate and marginally better efficiency metrics in a 3/4 seed matchup that historically favors the higher seed provide a thin path to victory in this tightly contested contest.

reasoningStep.title: Statistical Comparison and Health Assessment
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia and Tennessee are virtually identical in advanced metrics with KenPom rankings of 13 and 15 respectively and efficiency margins within 0.5 points. Virginia holds a decisive advantage in win-loss record at 29-4 versus Tennessee's 22-11, with both teams featuring elite top-15 defenses and comparable offensive profiles. Tennessee's Nate Ament has recovered from his high ankle sprain and played well in the SEC Tournament, while Virginia enters at full health with ACC championship credentials. The narrow statistical separation combined with Virginia's superior record and the historical 3-seed advantage in 3/4 matchups provides a slight edge to the Cavaliers.
evidence: Virginia KenPom rank 13, Torvik rank 14, adjEM 26.49, record 29-4
Tennessee KenPom rank 15, Torvik rank 15, adjEM 26.06, record 22-11
Tennessee's Nate Ament returned from ankle injury for SEC Tournament
Virginia enters NCAA Tournament at full health
